records.ety: [OE. dispreisen, OF. desprisier, despreisier, F. dpriser; pref. des- (L. dis-) + prisier, F. priser, to prize, praise. See Praise, and cf. Disprize, Depreciate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dispraised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Dispraising.]

defs: To withdraw praise from; to notice with disapprobation or some degree of censure; to disparage; to blame.
quotes: Dispraising the power of his adversaries.
I dispraised him before the wicked, that the wicked might not fall in love with him.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. despris. See Dispraise, v. t.]

defs: The act of dispraising; detraction; blame censure; reproach; disparagement.
quotes: In praise and in dispraise the same.
